Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mediates methylation of AtNIA2 and inhibits its transcriptional level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: epigenetic regulation study summarized in review
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CMT3-mediated DNA methylation represses AtNIA2; repression is suppressed by NH4+ treatment
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation when AtNIA2 repression is relieved
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CMT3 methylates AtNIA2 and represses its transcription. 2. NH4+ treatment suppresses this repression. 3. Epigenetic control therefore contributes to nitrate assimilation regulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chromatin-level regulator rather than pathway enzyme or TF.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential epigenetic target for tuning nitrate reductase expression.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mediates AtNIA2 Ser534 phosphorylation in vitro leading to reduced NR activity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: negative impact on NR activity when phosphorylation occurs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPK17 phosphorylates AtNIA2 at Ser534 in vitro. 2. This promotes 14-3-3 binding. 3. The modification reduces nitrate reductase activity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to relieve inhibitory NR phosphorylation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suggested to be involved in phosphorylation of AtNIA2
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: interaction study with SnRK1 beta subunits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK1 beta subunits interact with AtNIA2. 2. This suggests SnRK1 involvement in AtNIA2 phosphorylation. 3. It may thereby regulate nitrate reductase activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is suggestive rather than definitive.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory node for metabolic-state-dependent NR control.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: interacts with AtNIA1/AtNIA2 and dephosphorylates them to activate NR activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein interaction and dephosphorylation studies summarized in review
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; low temperature context for NR dephosphorylation in crops
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. B55, a PP2A structural subunit, interacts with AtNIA1 and AtNIA2. 2. It promotes their dephosphorylation. 3. This activates nitrate reductase activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PP2A-C2 catalytic subunit is also critical for NR activity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance active NR fraction.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The PP2A catalytic subunit C2 is critical for nitrate reductase activity. 2. It acts in the dephosphorylation machinery associated with NR. 3. It contributes to post-translational control of nitrate assimilation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing NR activation state.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: phosphorylates AtNIA2 Ser627 during later root development, increasing NR activity involved in H2O2-induced NO synthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: phosphorylation and functional studies summarized in review
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NO signaling and N metabolism linkage
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK6 phosphorylates AtNIA2 at Ser627. 2. This increases NR activity. 3. The modification participates in H2O2-induced NO synthesis during later root development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Post-translational regulator linking N metabolism and signaling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for context-dependent NR activation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SUMOylates AtNIA1/AtNIA2 and increases NR activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSIZ1 SUMOylates AtNIA1 and AtNIA2. 2. This modification increases NR activity. 3. SUMOylation is therefore an activating PTM in nitrate assimilation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SUMO sites were Lys356 in AtNIA1 and Lys353 in AtNIA2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive post-translational lever to boost NR activity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: interacts with AtNIA2 and may facilitate its activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNX1 interacts with AtNIA2. 2. Because CNX1 is a Moco biosynthesis enzyme, this interaction may facilitate AtNIA2 activity. 3. Cofactor biogenesis is thus linked to NR function.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Common gene because role is cofactor support, not direct pathway catalysis for target metabolite in paper framing.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target to support NR activity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: involved in phosphorylation-mediated modulation of AtGLN1.1 activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCRK3 is involved in phosphorylation of AtGLN1.1. 2. This modulates AtGLN1.1 activity. 3. Kinase-mediated PTM therefore affects GS function.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential handle for tuning GS1 activity.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: interacts with AtGLU1 in mitochondria and SHMT activity is regulated by AtGLU1 during photorespiration
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SHMT1 interacts with mitochondrial AtGLU1. 2. AtGLU1 regulates SHMT activity. 3. This connects nitrogen reassimilation during photorespiration with one-carbon metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a focal nitrogen-assimilation enzyme in this review section.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supportive target for coordinated photorespiratory engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots and leaves; nitrate inducible via NLP factors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for nitrate assimilation; contributes to NR activity and growth when nitrate is the nitrogen source
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports plant growth and nitrogen use
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate as N source; nitrate treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: nia1 nia2 double mutant shows severe growth retardation on nitrate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNIA1 encodes a nitrate reductase for the first step of nitrate assimilation. 2. Together with AtNIA2 it is required for normal NR activity and growth on nitrate. 3. It is transcriptionally activated by nitrate-response regulators such as NLP6/NLP7.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this review AtNIA1 is summarized as a major nitrate assimilation gene rather than a novel discovery.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve nitrate assimilation, though no direct breeding strategy summarized here.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major contributor to leaf NR activity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots and leaves; AtNIA2 mutation causes 90% decreased NR activity in leaves; light induced
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major nitrate reductase in leaves; mutation causes ~90% decreased NR activity in leaves and strong nitrate-assimilation defects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; enzyme activity assay; phosphorylation/SUMOylation studies
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: light; nitrate; ammonium affects methylation; H2O2 during later root development
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 14-3-3; CPK17; SnRK1 beta subunits; B55/PP2A; PP2A-C2; AtSIZ1; CNX1; MPK6
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss reduces growth on nitrate; strong perturbation of N metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNIA2 is the major nitrate reductase isoform in Arabidopsis leaves. 2. It is regulated transcriptionally by nitrate and light, and post-translationally by phosphorylation, dephosphorylation, SUMOylation, and DNA methylation-related control. 3. nia2 mutation strongly decreases NR activity, and nia1 nia2 double mutant is severely growth retarded on nitrate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser534 phosphorylation promotes 14-3-3 binding and reduces activity; Ser627 phosphorylation by MPK6 increases activity in NO synthesis context; CMT3-mediated methylation represses expression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning nitrate reduction efficiency, but multi-layer regulation suggests careful optimization is needed.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate inducible
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: functions in nitrite reduction during nitrate assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNiR1 catalyzes nitrite reduction to ammonium in nitrate assimilation. 2. Its expression is induced by nitrate. 3. It is transcriptionally activated by AtNLP6 and AtNLP7.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detailed crop characterization of NiR was stated to be limited.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible engineering target as part of coordinated nitrate assimilation enhancement.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key contributor to rice nitrate assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mainly expressed in node and leaf sheath
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports rice growth through nitrate assimilation; mutant shows decreased growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNR1.2 is a rice nitrate reductase expressed mainly in node and leaf sheath. 2. Loss of function decreases growth. 3. It is transcriptionally activated by DST.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented in review as a major crop NR gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for NUE improvement via enhanced nitrate reduction.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for NUE improvement in rice
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher in shoots than roots; nitrate-responsive via OsNLP4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: knockdown decreases tiller number and grain yield; overexpression or elite indica allele increases NR activity and grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knock-down; overexpression; natural variation/NIL; yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports growth, grain production, and NUE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNR2 is a major rice nitrate reductase affecting tiller number, grain yield, and NUE. 2. Knockdown reduces growth and yield, whereas overexpression increases yield. 3. The indica allele has higher NR activity than the japonica allele and improves performance when introduced into japonica.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly activated by OsNLP4 according to the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong breeding target; elite natural allele and transgenic overexpression both improve yield and NUE.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcription positively related to rice growth; activated by OsNLP4
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased expression is associated with increased panicle branches and grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNiR1 catalyzes nitrite reduction to ammonium in rice. 2. Higher OsNiR1 expression correlates with better growth and grain yield. 3. Promoter engineering with multiple nitrate response elements increased yield under both high and low N.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crop NiR functional characterization was noted as rare, but OsNiR1 is a clear exception.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for promoter engineering to improve NUE and grain yield.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine using glutamate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important isozyme for ammonium assimilation in leaves and roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in mesophyll cells of leaves under high nitrate; in root vasculature, epidermis and cortex; induced by excess ammonium and during leaf senescence; detected in developing seeds and phloem companion cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: critical for assimilation of ammonium derived from nitrate reduction, root ammonium assimilation, shoot reassimilation of transported ammonium, and N remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major transport and assimilation form of nitrogen supporting growth and seed filling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves; roots; developing seeds; phloem-associated tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high nitrate; excess ammonium; ammonium deficiency; leaf senescence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGLN1.2 is a central GS1 isozyme for primary ammonium assimilation in Arabidopsis leaves and roots. 2. It functions in nitrate-derived ammonium assimilation, transported ammonium assimilation, and nitrogen remobilization during senescence. 3. It acts redundantly with other GS1 isoforms under ammonium deficiency but is predominant under high ammonium.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser2 phosphorylation reported for AtGLN1.2 in phosphoproteomic study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve N assimilation and remobilization, likely requiring isoform-balanced engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine in plastids and mitochondria-associated photorespiratory context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major enzyme for photorespiratory ammonia reassimilation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced in roots by nitrate; active in mesophyll cells; GS activity in mitochondria and chloroplasts induced by photorespiratory stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: implements primary N assimilation in leaves with AtGLU1 and is essential for reassimilation of photorespiratory NH3; mutant is impaired under photorespiratory conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; subcellular localization; activity assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports growth, photorespiration, and carbon-nitrogen balance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate provision; photorespiratory stress; photorespiratory conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss impairs growth and N metabolism under photorespiration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGLN2 is a major plastidic/mitochondrial GS for leaf primary N assimilation and photorespiratory ammonia reassimilation. 2. Its activity is induced by photorespiratory stress and its loss impairs growth under photorespiratory conditions. 3. It acts in concert with AtGLU1 and is transcriptionally activated by AtNLP7.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser106 phosphorylation site was reported by phosphoproteomics.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to improve photorespiratory N recycling and nitrogen use efficiency.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transfers the amide group of glutamine to 2-oxoglutarate to generate glutamate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key component accounting for >95% total Fd-GOGAT activity in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: major leaf isoenzyme; induced/important in green tissue and photorespiration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential for primary N assimilation in leaves and for photorespiratory ammonia reassimilation; loss-of-function is chlorotic and lethal in air
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; subcellular localization; enzyme activity analysis; interaction assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports amino acid biosynthesis, photorespiration, and growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss causes chlorosis and lethality under permissive air conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGLU1 is the dominant Fd-GOGAT isozyme in Arabidopsis leaves. 2. It functions with AtGLN2 in primary N assimilation and photorespiratory NH3 reassimilation. 3. Loss of AtGLU1 causes severe chlorosis/lethality and the protein interacts with SHMT1 in mitochondria.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potential transcriptional target of GNC/CGA1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important but likely dosage-sensitive engineering target because severe defects occur upon dysfunction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: produces glutamate from glutamine and 2-oxoglutarate using NADH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important component for root ammonium assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: remarkably induced in roots by ammonium supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important component for NH4+ assimilation in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGLT encodes NADH-GOGAT involved in root ammonium assimilation. 2. Its expression is strongly induced by NH4+ in roots. 3. It is considered an important root component of the GS/GOGAT cycle.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving root ammonium assimilation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: predominantly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regarded as a factor responsible for primary N assimilation in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGLU2 is a root-predominant Fd-GOGAT. 2. It is regarded as contributing to primary nitrogen assimilation in roots. 3. Its function complements other GOGAT activities in different tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential root assimilation target.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: vital root ammonium-assimilation isozyme in rice
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by NH4+ in root epidermis; activated by GRF4 and OsMYB55
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: vital for ammonium assimilation in roots; overexpression enhances N assimilation but yield improvement is condition-dependent or negative depending on promoter/context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant and overexpression studies summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ammonium supply; high temperature response; different N conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: constitutive overexpression can decrease grain yield; growth-chamber-specific yield improvement under ubiquitin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;2 is a major rice GS1 isoform induced by ammonium in roots. 2. It is activated by regulators including GRF4 and OsMYB55. 3. Constitutive overexpression increases N assimilation but does not consistently improve yield, revealing a tradeoff between metabolism and agronomic performance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Important example that stronger N assimilation is not always translated into higher yield.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Requires context-specific promoter choice and trait balancing rather than simple constitutive overexpression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates photorespiratory ammonium into glutamine in chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major enzyme for photorespiratory ammonia reassimilation in rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: highly expressed in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for photorespiration and balanced C/N metabolism; dysfunction causes chlorosis and severe growth retardation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports photorespiration, growth, and N metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: dysfunction causes chlorosis and severe growth retardation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS2 is a major leaf GS2 enzyme in rice. 2. It is essential for reassimilation of photorespiratory ammonia. 3. Defects cause chlorosis and severe growth retardation, likely through disturbed photorespiration and C/N imbalance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conserved S-nitrosylation-related cysteine noted in review alignment.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving photorespiratory N recycling, with caution due to strong pleiotropy.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with OsNADH-GOGAT2 in phloem companion and phloem parenchyma cells of mature leaf blades
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mature leaf blades; roots also functionally relevant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important for ammonium assimilation and N remobilization; constitutive overexpression decreased grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant and overexpression studies summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;1 contributes to ammonium assimilation and leaf-to-sink nitrogen remobilization. 2. It is expressed in phloem-associated cells in mature leaves. 3. Constitutive overexpression reduced grain yield, indicating dosage or spatial-expression constraints.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not ideal for constitutive overexpression; spatially tailored deployment may be necessary.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine during grain development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important isozyme for grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high expression in spikelets; activated during endosperm development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: assumed to play an important role in grain filling and seed storage accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; transcriptional activation evidence by NF-YC12
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports grain development and seed storage deposition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGS1;3 is highly expressed in spikelets and implicated in rice grain filling. 2. It is activated by NF-YC12 during endosperm development. 3. The review links it to fine-tuning of seed storage accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PTM conservation suggests possible specialized regulation in developing grain.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising seed-specific engineering target for grain N accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: forms glutamate from glutamine and 2-oxoglutarate using NADH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: vital component of ammonium assimilation in roots and grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by NH4+ in root epidermis; high expression in spikelets
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: vital for ammonium assimilation; mutants accumulate free NH4+; native-promoter overexpression increases panicle/spikelet weight but activation tagging can reduce grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; overexpression/native promoter; activation tagging
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation and grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: activation tagging alone can reduce grain yield, while native promoter or co-activation with OsAMT1;2 improves yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNADH-GOGAT1 is essential for root ammonium assimilation and contributes to grain filling. 2. Mutants overaccumulate free ammonium, demonstrating its vital assimilation role. 3. Agronomic outcomes depend on expression context: native-promoter overexpression is beneficial, whereas constitutive activation alone can penalize yield.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly suppressed by Nhd1 according to review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target, but promoter and network context are critical.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: coexpressed with OsGS1;1 in phloem companion and phloem parenchyma cells of mature leaf blades
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: believed to mediate N remobilization during leaf senescence; disruption causes severe growth retardation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression localization; mutant disruption summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N remobilization to sink organs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNADH-GOGAT2 is implicated in N remobilization during rice leaf senescence. 2. It is coexpressed with OsGS1;1 in phloem-associated cells. 3. Disruption causes severe growth retardation, underscoring importance for N economy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving remobilization efficiency.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: generates glutamate from glutamine and 2-oxoglutarate in chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major enzyme for photorespiratory ammonia reassimilation in rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high expression in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for photorespiration and balanced C/N metabolism; dysfunction causes chlorosis and severe growth retardation; possible role in N translocation from senescing organs to grains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports photorespiration, amino acid synthesis and N remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: dysfunction causes chlorosis and severe growth retardation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFd-GOGAT1 is chloroplast-localized and highly expressed in rice leaves. 2. It is essential for photorespiratory NH3 reassimilation and C/N balance. 3. Mutant phenotypes and remobilization changes suggest roles in both leaf metabolism and N translocation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Fd-GOGAT activity is genetically connected to ARE1-mediated NUE effects.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indirect improvement may be achieved by relieving negative regulators such as ARE1.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: primary root nitrate metabolism; photorespiratory NH3 reassimilation in C4 leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major enzyme for ammonia assimilation in bundle sheath chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root expression induced by nitrate and activity detected only under nitrate provision; in bundle sheath chloroplasts of leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: roots under nitrate; leaves bundle sheath cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: catalyzes NH4+ derived from nitrate reduction in mesophyll/root contexts and is major enzyme for photorespiratory ammonia assimilation in C4 bundle sheath cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation and photorespiration in C4 plants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGLN2 is induced by nitrate in roots and active under nitrate provision. 2. In leaves it is localized in bundle sheath chloroplasts, fitting C4 photorespiratory compartmentation. 3. It is a major enzyme for assimilation of photorespiratory ammonia in maize.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conserved GS2 PTM sites are highlighted in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for optimizing C4 nitrogen assimilation and photorespiratory recycling.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: photorespiratory NH3 reassimilation in C4 leaves
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major enzyme for bundle sheath photorespiratory ammonia assimilation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: in chloroplasts of bundle sheath cells in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: major enzyme for ammonia assimilation released from photorespiration in C4 maize leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports photorespiration and amino acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmFd-GOGAT1 localizes to bundle sheath chloroplasts in maize leaves. 2. It functions with ZmGLN2 as a major system for photorespiratory NH3 assimilation in C4 plants. 3. Its role reflects cell-type specialization of photorespiration in maize.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for C4 leaf nitrogen-use optimization.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine for remobilization and kernel development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key factor for kernel number
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: stable detectable transcript in leaves after silking; mainly localized in mesophyll cells; constitutively expressed and linked to thousand-kernel-weight QTL
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential for maize production; mutations reduce kernel number and increased expression driven by pCsVMV increases kernels and grain yield by ~30%
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; promoter-driven overexpression; QTL colocalization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N remobilization and grain production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGLN1.3 is a key maize GS1 isoform for kernel production. 2. Mutation reduces kernel number, and overexpression increases kernels and grain yield by about 30%. 3. It also likely contributes to N remobilization during leaf senescence and grain filling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong translational evidence summarized in review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent engineering target for maize yield and NUE improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine during remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: transcript gradually increases in leaves after silking; localized in bundle sheath cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential but nonredundant role in maize production; mutation reduces kernel size
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N remobilization and kernel development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGLN1.4 accumulates during post-silking leaf senescence. 2. It localizes mainly in bundle sheath cells and has a nonredundant role distinct from ZmGLN1.3. 3. Mutation reduces kernel size, linking it to maize productivity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for kernel size/yield improvement, potentially complementary to ZmGLN1.3.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine in kernel-related tissues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: potential determinant of kernel growth
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically accumulated in kernel pedicel tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suggested potential role in kernel growth, with kernel-number QTL colocalization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGLN1.2 is specifically expressed in kernel pedicel tissue. 2. Its locus coincides with a kernel-number QTL. 3. The review suggests a role in maize kernel growth and developing grain nitrogen metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also noted to share conserved nitration-related residue with grain-associated GS1 clade.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for kernel-number improvement, although evidence is less direct than for ZmGLN1.3.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: leaf primary assimilation; remobilization to grains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important wheat GS2 isoform for NUE improvement
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate-induced in leaves; activated by TaNAC2-5A; elite allele overexpressed under native promoter improves N uptake and remobilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports leaf N assimilation and N remobilization to grain; transgenic expression of elite allele increases grain yield under low and high N
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression induction; overexpression under native promoter; field yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation, translocation and grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate; low and high nitrogen conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS2-2A is nitrate inducible and contributes to wheat nitrogen assimilation. 2. TaNAC2-5A activates TaGS2-2A. 3. The elite TaGS2-2Ab allele improves N uptake, N remobilization to grain, and grain yield under both low and high N.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homeolog-specific chromatin regulation was discussed mainly for TaGS2-2B, but TaGS2-2A is the direct breeding target in transgenic studies.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated high-value target for wheat NUE and yield improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important homeolog with highest expression among TaGS2 copies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: higher expression level than TaGS2-2A and TaGS2-2D due to active chromatin features
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suggested important function in wheat due to strongest expression regulated by promoter chromatin status
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: epigenetic/promoter accessibility analysis summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS2-2B shows higher expression than TaGS2-2A and TaGS2-2D. 2. Its stronger expression is associated with higher H3K4me3, lower cytosine methylation, and more open promoter chromatin. 3. The review suggests it has important functional weight among wheat GS2 homeologs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Epigenetic regulation is central for this homeolog.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential allele/chromatin target for tuning wheat GS2 expression.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root expression induced by ammonium; leaf expression increased at grain filling stage; mRNA mainly in parenchyma and mestome sheath around vascular bundles
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: roots and leaves during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: likely involved in root ammonium assimilation and N remobilization during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS1.1 is induced in roots by ammonium and in leaves during grain filling. 2. Its transcript localizes around vascular bundles, supporting a role in N remobilization. 3. The review assigns it to ammonium assimilation during N translocation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a homeologous gene family; specific homeolog effects not separated in text.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of source-to-grain N allocation improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: assimilates ammonium into glutamine for transport
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mainly localized around vessels of xylem in roots; in vascular phloem cells in leaves; increased expression in leaves at grain filling stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: possibly facilitates Gln loading into xylem and contributes to N remobilization during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrogen transport to shoot and grain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root xylem-associated tissues; leaf phloem cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS1.2 is localized near xylem in roots and phloem in leaves. 2. It likely facilitates glutamine loading and long-distance N translocation. 3. Its expression rises during grain filling, supporting a remobilization role.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Spatial localization is a key clue to function in transport-associated assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving N translocation efficiency.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: stably expressed candidate for grain development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root expression induced by ammonium; stably expressed during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suggested distinct role in grain development due to stable expression during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaGS1.3 is induced in roots by ammonium. 2. It shows stable expression during grain filling, suggesting a distinct role in grain development. 3. Conserved PTM-related residues suggest possible specialized post-translational control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review highlights conservation with other grain-associated GS1 orthologs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for grain-specific nitrogen metabolism engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts glutamine and 2-oxoglutarate to glutamate using NADH
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: predominant GOGAT activity during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: repressed by TabZIP60; high functional relevance during grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases spike number and grain yield; target of the repressor TabZIP60
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; regulator binding studies; enzyme activity and yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNADH-GOGAT1-3B is a major wheat GOGAT target for NUE improvement. 2. TabZIP60 represses its promoter. 3. Overexpression increases spike number and grain yield, while releasing repression boosts enzyme activity and productivity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review indicates NADH-GOGAT activity exceeds Fd-GOGAT activity during grain filling in wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target alone or via editing its repressor module.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes asparagine from aspartate using glutamine-derived amide nitrogen
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: critical for N utilization during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: critical for nitrogen utilization during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: nitrogen transport/storage form supporting seed development
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtASN1 encodes asparagine synthetase important for seed development. 2. It is critical for nitrogen utilization during this stage. 3. It is directly activated by bZIP11.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential seed nitrogen allocation target.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: participates in primary ammonium metabolism and N remobilization from source tissues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtASN2 participates in primary ammonium metabolism. 2. It is also involved in N remobilization from source tissues. 3. It has a function distinct from AtASN1 based on reciprocal expression patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for source-sink N redistribution.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes asparagine from aspartate and glutamine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important rice NUE target
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mainly expressed in root surface in NH4+-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: important for primary NH4+ assimilation; mutants show defective development; overexpression increases grain yield under N-limited condition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; overexpression under ubiquitin promoter; yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsASN1 is mainly expressed at the rice root surface in an ammonium-dependent manner. 2. Mutants show defective development, supporting a role in primary ammonium assimilation. 3. Overexpression increases grain yield under N limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong translational value highlighted in Table 2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance rice yield under low N.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential role in grain Asn synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high expression in grain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: plays essential role in grain Asn synthesis; mutant has lower free Asn concentration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaASN2 is highly expressed in wheat grain. 2. It is essential for free asparagine synthesis in grain. 3. Mutants show reduced free Asn concentration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Relevant to grain quality as well as N metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for manipulating grain free asparagine and quality traits.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ammonium assimilation and C/N stress response
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: reversibly interconverts glutamate and 2-oxoglutarate/ammonium, summarized here in N assimilation context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: essential under exogenous inorganic N supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: detected in root and shoot; induced by carbon starvation and exogenous ammonia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: essential role in N assimilation under exogenous inorganic N supply; mutant shows impaired growth
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: carbon starvation; exogenous ammonia; inorganic N supply
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: gdh1 mutant impaired under exogenous inorganic N supply
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGDH1 is expressed in roots and shoots and induced by C starvation and ammonia. 2. It is required for normal growth under exogenous inorganic nitrogen supply. 3. It functions redundantly with AtGDH2 under carbon starvation and is transcriptionally activated by STOP1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms homo- or heterohexamers with other GDHs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for stress-conditioned N assimilation tuning.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: NA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: detected in root and shoot; accumulates under long-term darkness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts redundantly with AtGDH1 under carbon starvation; target of EIN3 during anoxia-reoxygenation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: double mutant with gdh1 lowers survival under carbon starvation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGDH2 accumulates under long-term darkness. 2. It acts redundantly with AtGDH1 to support survival under carbon starvation. 3. It is regulated by stress-related transcription factors including STOP1 and EIN3.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Forms homo/heterohexamers with other GDHs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for stress-resilient nitrogen metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: primary nitrate response and nitrogen assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates expression of nitrate assimilation genes after nitrate treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: accumulates in nucleus after nitrate treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: master regulator that promotes nitrate assimilation gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of the primary nitrate response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene expression activation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation and growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNLP7 is described as the master regulator of the primary nitrate response. 2. After nitrate treatment it accumulates in the nucleus. 3. It activates AtNIA1, AtNIA2, AtNiR1, and AtGLN2 to promote nitrogen assimilation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key systems-level target for improving nitrate assimilation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates nitrate reductase and nitrite reductase genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes expression of major nitrate assimilation genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: important positive regulator of nitrate assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression activation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNLP6 upregulates AtNIA1, AtNIA2, and AtNiR1. 2. It functions similarly to AtNLP7 in nitrate-responsive transcription. 3. It positively regulates nitrate assimilation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for coordinated nitrate-assimilation engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsNR1.1; OsNR2; OsNiR1; N transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates nitrate assimilation and transporter genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases grain yield and NUE under different nitrogen conditions
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key transcriptional factor of rice NUE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct target analysis; overexpression; yield analysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports growth, N uptake and assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNLP4 directly activates major nitrate assimilation genes and N transporter genes. 2. It is highlighted as a key transcriptional factor for rice NUE. 3. Overexpression increases grain yield and NUE under low, normal, and high N.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice ortholog of Arabidopsis NLP7-like function.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for improving NUE via coordinated network activation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: increases ZmNiR1.1 expression by binding NRE in the 5' UTR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes nitrite reductase gene expression in maize
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator of a key nitrite reductase gene
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: binding and expression studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrate assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: cannot bind ZmNiR1.2 5'UTR even though NRE exists
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNLP5 activates ZmNiR1.1 by binding a nitrate response element in its 5' UTR. 2. This places it as a maize nitrate-response regulator. 3. It does not bind ZmNiR1.2 despite the presence of an NRE, indicating target specificity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Target selectivity is emphasized in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for fine-tuning specific nitrate-assimilation targets in maize.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds AtNIA2 promoter and promotes light-induced transcription
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mediates light-induced increase in NR transcript and activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct regulator linking light to nitrate reductase expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; promoter binding summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports light-coupled N assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HY5 binds the AtNIA2 promoter. 2. It contributes to light-induced AtNIA2 expression and increased NR activity. 3. hy5 hyh mutants impair this response.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful node for coordinating light and N assimilation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds AtNIA1 promoter and upregulates transcript level
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive regulation of nitrate reductase gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator of AtNIA1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding and expression studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrate assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP20 binds the AtNIA1 promoter. 2. It upregulates AtNIA1 transcript levels. 3. Therefore it positively regulates nitrate assimilation at the transcriptional level.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for targeted AtNIA1 activation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates OsNR1.2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates N assimilation through OsNR1.2 while being negatively related to drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-linked positive regulator of rice nitrate reduction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target activation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: positively regulates N assimilation but negatively related to drought tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DST activates OsNR1.2 transcription. 2. It positively regulates rice nitrogen assimilation. 3. This beneficial metabolic role is accompanied by a negative relationship with drought tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Illustrates tradeoff between NUE and stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target, but drought-related tradeoffs must be considered.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: endosperm development and seed storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates OsGS1;3 during endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: fine-tunes seed storage accumulation through activation of OsGS1;3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: endosperm-specific activator for grain N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transcriptional activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports seed storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NF-YC12 activates OsGS1;3 during rice endosperm development. 2. Through this it fine-tunes seed storage accumulation. 3. It represents tissue-specific regulation of grain nitrogen metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for endosperm-targeted engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GOGAT-mediated N assimilation and flowering-time linked N use
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: suppresses OsFd-GOGAT1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: links flowering-time regulation with repression of N assimilation gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key transcription factor regulating flowering time that suppresses a major GOGAT gene
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target suppression summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports amino acid synthesis and N economy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: possible growth/development tradeoff implied by flowering-time function
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Nhd1 suppresses OsFd-GOGAT1 expression. 2. It is a key transcription factor for rice flowering time. 3. Thus it connects developmental timing with nitrogen assimilation control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential coupling point between heading date and NUE that may require careful balancing.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: AtGLU1 is a potential target
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrate; cytokinin/carbon-related signals implied by gene name context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: potentially promotes expression of key green-tissue N assimilation gene
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: potential regulator of the key green-tissue GOGAT gene
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target inference summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation in green tissue
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GNC is a GATA transcription factor implicated in nitrogen/carbon-related control. 2. AtGLU1 is identified as a potential target. 3. It may help regulate N assimilation in green tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is described as potential rather than definitive.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream regulator, though validation depth is limited in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: AtGLU1 is a potential target
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: potential regulator of key N assimilation gene in green tissue
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: potential activator of AtGLU1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target inference summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports green tissue N assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CGA1/GNL is a GATA transcription factor paralog of GNC. 2. AtGLU1 is listed as a potential target. 3. It may participate in regulation of nitrogen assimilation in green tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in review is potential rather than direct functional dissection.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible network regulator for future engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly binds promoter of TaNADH-GOGAT1-3B to repress expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: knock-down increases NADH-GOGAT activity and grain yield
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: repressor of N utilization in wheat
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding; RNAi knock-down; enzyme activity and yield analysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports grain production via improved N assimilation when repression is relieved
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TabZIP60 directly represses TaNADH-GOGAT1-3B. 2. Knock-down strongly increases NADH-GOGAT activity. 3. Releasing this repression raises spike number and grain yield, making TabZIP60 a negative regulator of NUE.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clear translational evidence through RNAi and target overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent gene-editing/RNAi target to improve wheat NUE and yield.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds TaGS2-2A promoter and activates expression; also upregulates nitrate transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: overexpression increases N uptake/assimilation and grain yield under low and high N
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: cereal-specific nitrate-responsive activator improving NUE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding; overexpression; yield analysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation and grain production
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low N and high N conditions; nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaNAC2-5A is a nitrate-inducible NAC transcription factor. 2. It directly activates TaGS2-2A and nitrate transporter genes. 3. Overexpression enhances N uptake/assimilation and increases grain yield under both low and high N.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to cereal-specific clade with OsNAC19 and ZmNAC67 suggested to have conserved functions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong regulator target for wheat NUE improvement.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates AtGLN1.3 and other N-assimilation genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: integrates circadian rhythm with N assimilation gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key factor accounting in part for circadian N assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports time-of-day coordination of N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCA1 directly activates AtGLN1.3 and other N-assimilation genes. 2. It links the circadian clock to nitrogen assimilation. 3. This provides a mechanism for circadian N metabolism regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for temporal optimization of N assimilation.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: integration of N assimilation, carbon fixation, and growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates OsGS1;2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes N assimilation gene expression while integrating growth and carbon fixation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key integrator of N assimilation, carbon fixation, and rice growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation studies summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation and growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GRF4 activates OsGS1;2 expression. 2. It integrates nitrogen assimilation, carbon fixation, and rice growth. 3. Thus it is a higher-order regulator of N-use efficiency networks.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Attractive systems-level breeding target for coordinated yield and NUE improvement.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly binds the promoter region and activates OsGS1;2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: links heat response to increased expression of a major N assimilation gene
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: stress-responsive activator of OsGS1;2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter binding and activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N assimilation under stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB55 is a rice R2R3-MYB involved in high-temperature response. 2. It directly activates OsGS1;2. 3. This suggests a connection between heat stress response and nitrogen assimilation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for maintaining N assimilation under heat stress.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: asparagine biosynthesis and sugar-signaling-linked N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates AtASN1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes asparagine synthesis gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: direct activator linking sugar signaling to Asn biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports N storage/transport and seed development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP11 directly activates AtASN1. 2. It plays an important role in sugar signaling. 3. Thus it couples carbon/sugar status to asparagine biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for carbon-nitrogen coordination in seed N metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly activates GDH1 and GDH2 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: regulates aluminum resistance via activation of GDH genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: stress-responsive controller of GDH-dependent N metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: direct activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports stress adaptation and N/C metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STOP1 directly activates GDH1 and GDH2. 2. It regulates aluminum resistance in Arabidopsis. 3. This implicates GDH-mediated nitrogen metabolism in stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for coupling stress resistance with N metabolism.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GDH-related N metabolism during oxygen stress recovery
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: GDH2 is one target during anoxia-reoxygenation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: links ethylene signaling to GDH2-dependent metabolic adjustment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master ethylene regulator controlling GDH2 under stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: target identification summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports metabolic recovery under oxygen stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EIN3 targets GDH2 during anoxia-reoxygenation. 2. It is the master regulator of ethylene signaling. 3. This places GDH-linked N metabolism under hormonal stress control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for stress-responsive metabolic resilience.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: knockout recovers decreased Fd-GOGAT activity in osfd-gogat1 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: knockout increases grain number per panicle and 10-20% grain yield under N limitation; elite alleles improve low-N yield
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: down-regulator of rice NUE
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout; natural variation/NIL analysis; field yield analysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports improved N assimilation when negative regulation is removed
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARE1 is a negative regulator of rice NUE. 2. Knockout restores Fd-GOGAT activity and increases grain yield under N limitation. 3. Elite ARE1 haplotypes from 9311 and MH63 improve yield in low-N near-isogenic lines.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly conserved among plants; knockout of TaARE1 also increases wheat grain yield.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target for knockout/editing to enhance NUE under low N.
